questions.q: According to reports, Prime Minister Mark Carney's Liberals are in a position to win a majority government in byelections held on April 13, 2026?
options: Yes, byelection results on April 13, 2026, indicated the Liberals could secure a majority government.
No, the Liberals lost the byelections held on April 13, 2026.
The byelections were postponed due to unforeseen circumstances.
The results of the April 13, 2026, byelections have not yet been released.
questions.correctText: Yes, byelection results on April 13, 2026, indicated the Liberals could secure a majority government.

questions.q: Is cancer still the leading cause of death in Canada, with new studies indicating a rise in multiple types as of April 2026?
options: Yes, cancer remains the top cause of death in Canada, and recent studies show an increase in various types.
No, heart disease has surpassed cancer as the leading cause of death in Canada.
Recent studies indicate a significant decline in all types of cancer deaths in Canada.
Cancer is no longer among the top causes of death in Canada due to advancements in treatment.
questions.correctText: Yes, cancer remains the top cause of death in Canada, and recent studies show an increase in various types.

questions.q: Has Prime Minister Mark Carney scheduled a meeting with Finnish President Alexander Stubb in Ottawa during the week of April 13, 2026?
options: Yes, Prime Minister Carney is set to meet with Finnish President Alexander Stubb in Ottawa this week.
No, the meeting between Prime Minister Carney and President Stubb has been canceled.
President Stubb will be meeting with the Canadian opposition leader, not the Prime Minister.
The meeting is scheduled to take place in Helsinki, not Ottawa.
questions.correctText: Yes, Prime Minister Carney is set to meet with Finnish President Alexander Stubb in Ottawa this week.

questions.q: Is there a proposal being discussed at the Liberal convention to implement a $500,000 exit tax for educated Canadians, as suggested by tech executive Terry Newman?
options: Yes, tech executive Terry Newman has pitched a $500,000 exit tax for educated Canadians at the Liberal convention.
No, the proposed exit tax is for low-income Canadians, not educated ones.
The Liberal convention rejected the proposal for an exit tax outright.
The proposed exit tax is $50,000, not $500,000.
questions.correctText: Yes, tech executive Terry Newman has pitched a $500,000 exit tax for educated Canadians at the Liberal convention.
